attempt.claim: Erdos #124: lean/Vela/Erdos124IntervalBlock.lean kernel-verifies the interval-block criterion that underlies the GPT-Pro reduction (att_61ff8f87c2f692b0). interval_block: if PB is L-syndetic (every length-L window meets it), PF contains the L consecutive integers u..u+L-1, and PB+PF subset PS, then PS contains every n >= u+L; interval_block_eventually gives the cofinite (atTop) form. Instantiated with PB=Sigma(B), PF=Sigma(F), PS=Sigma(B union F) for a disjoint split, this turns the open #124 target (erdos124.ne_zero) into the interval-block lemma. The interval-block lemma itself (existence of such a split for every admissible finite D,k) is the open core and is NOT proved here. Build green; axioms = {propext, Quot.sound} (+Classical.choice for the atTop form); no sorry.
